question_en: My child got very low marks in 10th/12th. Is their career over before it started?
question_hi_hinglish: Mere bacche ke 10th/12th mein bahut kam marks aaye hain. Kya uska career shuru hone se pehle hi khatam ho gaya?
answer_en: Absolutely not. In 2026, marks are just one way to measure memory, but the modern world measures 'Skill.' Many high-paying roles like Video Editing, UI/UX Design, and Digital Marketing don't look at your report card—they look at your 'Portfolio' (the work you have actually done). A child with 50% marks but 100% passion for a skill can earn more than a topper.
answer_hi_hinglish: Bilkul nahi! 2026 mein marks sirf yaadash napne ka tarika hai, par asli duniya 'Hunar' (Skill) dekhti hai. Video Editing, Design, aur Digital Marketing jaise bade careers mein koi aapki marksheet nahi dekhta—wo sirf aapka kaam dekhte hain. 50% marks wala baccha bhi agar apne kaam mein mahir hai, toh wo topper se zyada kama sakta hai.

question_en: If companies don't look at marks, how do they decide who to hire?
question_hi_hinglish: Agar companies marks nahi dekhti, toh wo kaise tay karti hain ki kise naukri deni hai?
answer_en: They look for 'Proof of Work' or a Portfolio. This is a digital folder of projects the child has completed. For example, if your child wants to be a coder, they show the apps they built. If they want to be a social media manager, they show the accounts they grew. In 2026, 'showing' is more powerful than 'telling' your marks.
answer_hi_hinglish: Wo 'Proof of Work' ya Portfolio dekhte hain. Ye un kaamo ki list hoti hai jo bacche ne khud kiye hain. Jaise, agar baccha coder banna chahta hai, toh wo apne banaye huye apps dikhata hai. 2026 mein 'kaam karke dikhana' marks batane se kahin zyada asardar hai. Aapka portfolio hi aapki asli degree hai.

question_en: My child hates books but loves fixing things or being creative. Is there a place for them?
question_hi_hinglish: Mera baccha kitabein pasand nahi karta, par use cheezein theek karna ya creativity pasand hai. Kya uske liye koi jagah hai?
answer_en: Yes! These children are 'Kinesthetic Learners'—they learn by doing. In 2026, roles like Robotic Maintenance, Specialized Pastry Chefs, and 3D Modelers are perfect for them. These jobs value 'hand-brain coordination' over 'book-reading.' Their struggle with exams just means they are built for practical success, not theoretical testing.
answer_hi_hinglish: Haan! Aise bacche 'Practical Learners' hote hain jo kaam karke seekhte hain. 2026 mein Robotic Maintenance, 3D Modeling, aur Specialized Chefs jaise roles unke liye best hain. In kaamo mein 'kitabi gyan' se zyada 'hath aur dimaag ki furti' chahiye hoti hai. Exams mein kam number ka matlab ye nahi ki wo kamzor hain, bas wo asli kaam ke liye bane hain.

question_en: If my child can't get into a top college due to low marks, what is the alternative?
question_hi_hinglish: Agar kam marks ki wajah se acche college mein admission na mile, toh dusra rasta kya hai?
answer_en: The alternative is 'Micro-Credentials' and specialized Diplomas. In 2026, taking a 6-month intensive course from an industry-recognized platform is often better than a 4-year degree from a weak college. These courses focus 100% on job skills. Many 'Pehchaan' students skip traditional colleges to join these 'Fast-Track' programs and start earning by age 19.
answer_hi_hinglish: Iska rasta hai 'Specialized Diplomas' aur short-term courses. 2026 mein kisi kamzor college se 4 saal ki degree lene se behtar hai ki 6 mahine ka koi aisa course kiya jaye jo seedha naukri dila sake. Ye courses sirf wahi sikhate hain jo office mein kaam aata hai. Bahut se bacche 19 saal ki umar mein hi aisi training lekar kamana shuru kar dete hain.

question_en: My child has lost confidence because of bad results. How do I bring them back?
question_hi_hinglish: Result kharab aane se mere bacche ka hosla toot gaya hai. Use wapas kaise sambhalun?
answer_en: Change the goalpost. Tell them: 'One exam cannot define your 40-year career.' Show them examples of successful people who failed in school but led industries. Introduce them to a 'Pehchaan' skill like Graphic Design or Drone Piloting. When they see they can do a modern task successfully, their confidence will return faster than any lecture could.
answer_hi_hinglish: Unse kahein: 'Ek exam aapke 40 saal ke career ko tay nahi kar sakta.' Unhe un logo ki misal dein jo school mein fail huye par duniya mein kamyab hain. Unhe 'Pehchaan' ka koi naya hunar dikhayein, jaise Graphic Design ya Drone chalana. Jab wo dekhenge ki wo ek naya kaam acche se kar sakte hain, toh unka confidence apne aap wapas aa jayega.

question_en: Is it true that even big companies like Google and Tata now hire without looking at degrees?
question_hi_hinglish: Kya ye sach hai ki Google aur Tata jaisi badi companies ab bina degree ke bhi naukri deti hain?
answer_en: Yes, it is a 2026 reality. Many global giants have removed the 'Degree Required' tag. They now use 'Aptitude Tests' and 'Skill Challenges.' If your child can solve the problem they give, they get the job, regardless of their school marks. They value 'Can you do the work?' over 'What was your score in History?'
answer_hi_hinglish: Haan, ye 2026 ki sachai hai. Bahut si badi companies ne 'Degree' ki shart hata di hai. Wo ab 'Skill Tests' leti hain. Agar aapka baccha unka diya hua task solve kar leta hai, toh use naukri mil jati hai, chahe school mein marks kitne bhi hon. Unke liye 'kaam aana' sabse bada marksheet hai.

question_en: Without high marks, they can't get a Government Job. Isn't that a big loss?
question_hi_hinglish: Kam marks ke bina sarkari naukri nahi milegi. Kya ye bada nuksan nahi hai?
answer_en: Government jobs are one path, but in 2026, the Private and Freelance sectors offer 10 times more opportunities and often higher salaries. While a government job offers 'safety,' the new-age skills offer 'growth.' A skilled person can increase their income every year, whereas a fixed salary might stay the same. Low marks in school don't close the door to wealth.
answer_hi_hinglish: Sarkari naukri sirf ek rasta hai, par 2026 mein Private sector aur Freelancing mein 10 guna zyada mauke hain aur salary bhi zyada hai. Sarkari naukri mein 'suraksha' hai, toh naye hunar mein 'tarakki' hai. Ek hunar-mand baccha har saal apni kamai badha sakta hai. School ke kam marks paise kamane ka rasta nahi rokte.

question_en: Is an ITI or Vocational course better for a 'low-marks' student than a simple BA/BCom?
question_hi_hinglish: Kya kam marks wale bacche ke liye BA/BCom karne se behtar ITI ya koi vocational course hai?
answer_en: In 2026, absolutely. A simple BA/BCom often leads to unemployment because it doesn't teach a specific 'sellable' skill. But an ITI in 'EV Mechanic' or a vocational course in 'Digital Animation' makes the child ready for a job immediately. It is better to be a 'Skilled Professional' than an 'Unemployed Graduate.'
answer_hi_hinglish: 2026 mein bilkul! Khali BA/BCom karne se aksar naukri nahi milti kyunki usme koi khaas hunar nahi hota. Par agar baccha EV (Electric Vehicle) Mechanic ya Digital Animation jaisa course kare, toh wo turant kamane layaq ban jata hai. 'Berozgaar Graduate' banne se behtar hai ki baccha 'Hunar-mand Professional' bane.
